envelope_assembly: Project-specific; durable waterfront/institutional envelope where applicable

sources.title: Time-Saver Standards for Building Types — Restaurants and Eating Places, Seating, Serving Units, Kitchens
NFPA 96 (referenced conceptually only for commercial kitchen exhaust / fire suppression)

small.name: Neighborhood Restaurant
small.notes: Dining room, bar optional, prep kitchen

medium.name: Urban Food Hall
medium.notes: Multiple vendor stalls, common seating, commissary/back-of-house

large.name: Destination Market Hall
large.notes: Multiple vendors, event zone, significant support and storage

space_library.name: Entry / Queue
notes: Front-door queue and waiting can dominate peak use.

space_library.name: Host / POS / Check Stand
notes: Keep line management out of primary egress paths.

space_library.name: Dining / Common Seating
notes: Use operator-specific seat density later; keep aisles and service paths separated.

space_library.name: Expo / Service Station
notes: Connects kitchen to dining and reduces cross-traffic.

space_library.name: Kitchen / Vendor Prep
notes: Detailed equipment loads and line lengths intentionally deferred until operator is known.
Follow local code and health department requirements for handwash, warewash, and food flow.

space_library.name: Dish / Warewash
notes: Separate dirty and clean flow as operator planning advances.

space_library.name: Walk-in Cooler / Freezer
notes: Cold-chain strategy depends on operator model.

space_library.name: Receiving / Refuse
notes: Keep waste and receiving separate from public approach where feasible.

space_library.name: Public Restrooms
notes: Fixture counts to be finalized in code pass.
